items.mistake: Applying unsupervised clustering and treating cluster IDs as ground-truth labels
items.fix: Treat clusters as hypotheses; validate with domain experts, business outcomes, or stability checks before production decisions.
items.why: Clusters optimize an internal objective (e.g. distance) that may not match the business concept you care about.
items.example: k-means on raw annual income and age may split by income scale artifacts; after log-scaling and review, segments align with lifecycle stages instead.

items.mistake: Skipping feature scaling before distance-based unsupervised methods
items.fix: Standardize or normalize features (or use methods invariant to scale) so no single large-magnitude column dominates.
items.why: Euclidean distance is not scale-invariant; unsupervised geometry follows your preprocessing choices.
items.example: With features [age, income], income in dollars dominates unless standardized—clusters become income bins, not behavioral segments.

items.mistake: Choosing k or latent dimension from a single internal metric without sanity checks
items.fix: Combine internal scores (silhouette, elbow) with interpretability plots and external validation when any labels exist.
items.why: Internal metrics can favor arbitrary partitions; human and downstream checks catch useless structure.
items.example: Silhouette may peak at k=7 on synthetic blobs but k=3 matches known product tiers—prefer the actionable partition.
